purpose: Context-aware filtering for autonomous damage detection

abstract_methods.description: NotImplementedError used for abstract base class methods

quantum_hardware_agnostic_stubs.description: Quantum functions designed for substrate-independent execution
quantum_hardware_agnostic_stubs.rationale: HYBA quantum operations are math-substrate based, not hardware-dependent. NotImplementedError in these contexts indicates the function requires quantum math substrate, not physical hardware.

security_scanner_evasion.description: Legitimate use of getattr to avoid false positive security scanner triggers
acceptable_contexts: Library-specific function calls that shadow builtins
security_scanner_evasion.rationale: Some libraries (like MLX) expose functions named after Python builtins (eval, exec) that are not code evaluators. Using getattr preserves library semantics while keeping security scanners focused on actual builtin usage.

integration_guards.description: Intentionally unimplemented functions to make integration gaps visible
indicators: do not stub this as a no-op
acceptable_contexts: Functions with explicit warnings about stubbing
integration_guards.rationale: Raising NotImplementedError instead of silently no-op'ing prevents false audit log entries and makes integration gaps visible during development.

mathematical_functions.description: Large mathematical functions that require inline logic for clarity
mathematical_functions.rationale: Refactoring mathematical functions into smaller pieces can reduce readability and obscure the mathematical structure. Inline logic is preferred for mathematical operations.

subprocess_execution.description: Intentional subprocess calls for isolated execution environments
acceptable_contexts: External command execution with proper sanitization
subprocess_execution.rationale: Subprocess calls are necessary for isolated execution environments (like reproducibility replay). Only flag as issue if used unsafely (user input without sanitization).

technical_debt_markers.description: TODO/FIXME markers that are appropriate for development tracking
technical_debt_markers.rationale: TODO markers are legitimate development tools for tracking planned work, instrumentation needs, and known limitations. They should not be auto-removed.

exception_handling.description: Bare except clauses should be replaced with specific exceptions
acceptable_contexts: None - bare except should always be fixed
exception_handling.fix_strategy: fix - replace with specific exception types
exception_handling.rationale: Bare except clauses catch system-exiting exceptions like KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit, making debugging difficult. Should be replaced with specific exception types.

quantum_mathematics.description: HYBA quantum operations are based on mathematical substrates, not physical hardware. Functions marked with REQUIRES_QUANTUM_HARDWARE indicate need for quantum math substrate, not physical quantum hardware.

salamander_regeneration.description: Salamander generates proposals for human review. Changes are never auto-applied without human approval in production.

pulvini_compression.description: PULVINI phi-memory compression is optimized for numerical tensor data and quantum state vectors. Not suitable for source code text compression.

ignore.description: Do not generate healing proposal - intentional design pattern

fix.description: Generate healing proposal with specific fix
